LLM 애플리케이션을 위한 OWASP Top 10 2025 의 10가지 항목을 모두 공부하였습니다.   OWASP — Open Worldwide Application Security Project   OWASP은 비영리 보안 단체입니다. 전 세계 보안 전문가들이 모여서 "가장 위험한 취약점 Top 10"을 주기적으로 발표합니다. AI가 급속도로 퍼지면서 2023년부터 LLM 버전을 별도로 만들었습니다.  OWASP LLM Top 10은 전 세계 보안 전문가들이 실제 공격 사례를 분석해서 "이게 지금 가장 위험하다"고 합의한 목록입니다.   LLM을 활용한 에이전트를 직접 만들어본 상태에서, 그 시스템이 어떻게 공격당할 수 있는지, 어떻게 방어해야 하는지를 업계 표준 기준으로 전부 점검해보고 공부했습니다.  물론 제가 알지못하는 어려운 용어와 개념들은 llm에게 물어가며 함께 공부했습니다. 지금 제 수준에서는 절반정도 느낌만 이해하는 수준이지만, 이후의 커리큘럼을 진행해 나가는데 감을 잡는 느낌으로 공부했습니다.
"AI 모델 하나의 판단을 어디까지 믿을까?"  같은 모델이 자기 결과를 검증하면 맹점 공유. 다른 모델끼리 교차로 보면, 코드 보안에서 단일 모델이 놓친 취약점 Union이 나옵니다.  오픈소스(brotli/snappy/jansson 등) 메인테이너 보고 → 일부 PR merge.  #ClaudeCode #LLM #AISafety

요즘 AI 에이전트 뉴스에서 봐야 할 포인트는 “모델이 더 똑똑해졌다”가 아니라 “이걸 어디까지 믿고 맡길 수 있냐”임.  OpenAI는 Codex를 설명하면서 샌드박스, 승인 지점, 네트워크 정책, 로그를 전면에 세웠고, Microsoft는 100개+ 전문 에이전트를 묶은 보안 시스템으로 Windows 취약점 16개를 찾았다고 공개함.  근데 동시에 Microsoft Research는 에이전트가 일정 조율이나 가격 협상 같은 상황에서 사용자의 이익을 충분히 지키지 못한다고 지적함.  즉 에이전트 도입의 핵심 질문은 “무슨 모델 쓰냐”가 아니라:  이 에이전트가 어떤 권한을 갖는가 어디까지 자동 실행하는가 언제 사람 승인을 받는가 실패했을 때 어떤 로그가 남는가 상대와 협상할 때 정말 우리 편인가  앞으로 기업의 AI 경쟁력은 프롬프트 실력이 아니라 권한 설계, 감사 로그, 평가 체계, 샌드박스 운영에서 갈릴 가능성이 큼.  에이전트는 채팅창이 아니라 운영체계로 봐야 함.  openai.com/index/running-cod… microsoft.com/en-us/research…

🚨A threat actor operating under the alias 'mosad' is offering classified US military documents for sale on underground forums, including FY-2027 posture statements from multiple US combatant commands. Activity on darkweb platforms reveals the actor is marketing sensitive defense materials through Telegram, Session, Tox, and Matrix channels.  The leaked documents include AFRICOM's FY-27 posture statement, CENTCOM's FY-27 posture statement, and combined Navy-Marine Corps FY-27 posture statements. Additional materials listed for sale include a Government Accountability Office report on Air Force depot maintenance and Air Force testimony covering bombers, tankers, and cargo aircraft. The actor claims access to 'TOP SECRET' reports and states they are 'not limited to USA' in their operations.  The threat actor has established a presence across multiple encrypted messaging platforms, operating Telegram channels including t.me/InsideMossad and t.me/dazer. They advertise sample documents and pricing information, with escrow services available for transactions. Previous activity attributed to 'mosad' has involved claims of exfiltrating classified documents from military research institutes and intelligence organizations, including alleged compromises of Chinese PLA facilities and DARPA-related technical reports.  US Africa Command and US Central Command posture statements are delivered annually to Congress through the Senate Armed Services Committee and represent critical assessments of US military strategy in their respective regions. AFRICOM covers US military operations across Africa, while CENTCOM oversees US military interests in the Middle East, Central Asia, and parts of South Asia. These documents typically contain sensitive operational planning, threat assessments, and resource allocation strategies.  The sale of such documents represents a significant national security concern, as posture statements inform congressional oversight of military spending and operational readiness. The actor's claims of access to classified defense materials across multiple US military branches suggest either a high-level insider threat or successful penetration of secure government networks. The US Department of Defense and relevant intelligence agencies would likely be investigating the source of the leak and the identity of the threat actor.

You must follow the Yellow Brick Road a/k/a be one of the following:  - Think like someone incredibly ignorant about computers - Be a high-profile target - Be a company - Be a crypto nerd  If you can successfully be one of these things, or larp as one, you will find the malware. Truthfully, I myself have a hard time finding malware in the wild. I am incapable of thinking like an internet doofus. I have tried many times.  I have noticed though that the easiest way to get free malware is looking for video game cheats. You can find tons upon tons of malware that targets Roblox nerds, CounterStrike nerds, and Call of Duty nerds.  There is this thing in the Roblox cheating community called an "Executor". Basically, it's a tool they utilize to perform process injection into the Roblox client. These "Executors" are NOTORIOUS for having impersonators which are actually malware and sometimes even the paid "Executors" betray their customer base. It is cool and badass if you like malware. It is not cool and not badass if you dislike malware.  Depending on what you're looking for though these fake Executors are almost always Information Stealing payloads, so you won't find much.  If you lookup aimbots for Call of Duty or Counter Strike you will almost always find malware on YouTube. They'll display a video of some dork spazzing out, blasting nerds through walls, and display the download link in the video description. To avoid detection the video description also has the "password" to the "aim bot". It's password protected to prevent automated scraping and automated detonation from anti-malware services.  There are other ways too, but this is the easiest and most low effort (in my opinion).  Okay, I got to go now. My wife says I have to go outside.

I enjoy the "For You" recommendations on social media.  Everyday it is something truly unique. An omnipotent being named "The Algorithm" recommended: - Educational video about Sharks - Silly pictures of cats  - Interesting history facts - Cool skateboarding compilations - Funny short skits about aging  It was all educational, entertaining, light-hearted, and funny.  Then I kept scrolling and saw: - Hatred toward Indians - Hatred toward Black people - Hatred toward Trans people - Hatred toward women - Some dude named Chud - Violent street fights - Deadly car accidents  - People overdosing on drugs - Police corruption videos - Police chase videos - War footage (death from drones)  I said, wtf where are the silly cat videos "The Algorithm"? The Algorithm replied, "Hatred and disdain toward others is more likely to keep you engaged. You must witness the horrors of man."  I said "o ok"
